harnessRegression.summary: Harness regression passed with 16 cases covering redesign warning mode, hardcoded color blockers, layout blockers, expired design-exempt, token sync drift, semantic slot metadata, and visualReview evidence.

harnessValidate.summary: Harness validation passed after adding designMode schema, semantic slot metadata requirements, and guardTokenSync as a known guard step.

tokenSync.summary: Token sync guard passed after aligning documented glass-* tokens with allowed Tailwind color prefixes and ignoring forbidden-example token names.

documentationReview.summary: Documented maintenance/redesign/token-migration modes, visualReview evidence, token sync drift checks, structured design-exempt metadata, and durable semantic slot metadata.

guardVerify.summary: Harness validation, token sync guard, design guard, diff guard, lint, typecheck, full Vitest suite, SQL contract check, and evidence guard passed.

issuesObserved: The previous design guard treated all UI work as maintenance and could block broad redesign exploration on color prefix choice rather than durable quality risks.
missedEvidence: No browser runtime evidence is required because this change only updates Harness scripts, rules, fixtures, schema, and docs.
harnessImprovementProposal: Keep design guard severity mode-aware: maintenance remains strict, redesign allows exploratory color warnings, and durable accessibility/layout/token drift risks remain blockers.
retrospective.reason: The change generalizes a repeated Agent-process failure during broad UI/UX redesign work into durable harness behavior.
